key_insight: Latent Drift predicts slow disease progression by modeling subtle anatomical change in a compressed latent space instead of trying to regenerate full MRI anatomy directly.
clinical_relevance: By better forecasting future brain changes in neurodegenerative disease, the approach could support earlier intervention planning and improve how longitudinal imaging is used in clinical trials.
method: Learns progression in a latent semantic representation to reduce distraction from stable baseline anatomy.
Uses Finite Scalar Quantization to suppress small high-frequency noise while preserving consistent structural drift.
Targets low-signal longitudinal MRI where standard generative models struggle with identity collapse and diffuse spurious changes.
evidence_signal: In longitudinal 3D brain MRI experiments, Latent Drift outperformed diffusion and autoregressive transformer baselines on both generative fidelity and clinically relevant evaluation metrics.
watch_item: Results are shown on longitudinal brain MRI, so broader validation is still needed before assuming the method generalizes across diseases, scanners, and imaging protocols.
paper_title: Progression as Latent Drift: Generative Forecasting of Slow-Evolving Pathologies
